title: Cirrus: Remove hardcoded snap version
body:
Should fix #4134.

`git` version semantics is described at https://snapcraft.io/docs/snapcraft-yaml-reference

Hi @abitrolly. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
I run out of ideas. Asked here https://forum.snapcraft.io/t/the-go-plugin/8505/7?u=abitrolly
/ok-to-test
(re-ran failed test snap task)

@cevich it won't help. It is either `snapcraft` go plugin not setting all paths correctly, or it could be that `libpod` biuld is more complicated and needs more processing than required by standard `go` builds supported by `snapcraft`.

The build from tagged source was successful, and I think the problem is with `snapcraft` and source path. The workaround is to reuse the binary from the build task, but I don't see how to do this yet in Cirrus CI.

@abitrolly thanks for taking a look.  Hmmm, so `snapcraft` must not be using the Makefile then?

Re: packaging based on the release zip/tar files we upload for each testing task.  It is possible to retrieve them during either/both `test_building_snap` and `upload_snap` (assuming some `depends_on` adjustments so they happen right before 'success').

For both for PRs and on 'master', we use fixed base URLs (see `<BRANCH  |  PR>` below)

* `https://storage.googleapis.com/libpod-pr-releases/` for PRs
* `https://storage.googleapis.com/libpod-master-releases/` for master

The filenames uploaded for any build can be discovered programmaticly, given a clean tree (no added/removed commits).  The file names use the format:

```
<BASENAME | REMOTENAME>-<VERSION>-<BRANCH  |  PR>-<DIST>-<ARCH>.gz
```

Where:
* `<BASENAME | REMOTENAME>` is the value from `hack/get_release_info.sh` with one of those keywords as an argument.  Currently "podman" or "podman-remote"
* `<VERSION>` is the value from `hack/get_release_info.sh VERSION`
* `<BRANCH>` is the value of `$CIRRUS_BRANCH` (if `$CIRRUS_PR` is an empty string)
* `<PR>` is the string 'pr' followed by the value of '$CIRRUS_PR` (if NOT an empty string)
* `<DIST>` is the value from `hack/get_release_info.sh DIST`.  Currently "fedora", "ubuntu", "windows" or "darwin"
* `<ARCH>` is the value from `hack/get_release_info.sh ARCH`

Alternatively, all the above (except `<BRANCH | PR>`) can be parsed from the `make release.txt` output file.

HTH
This pull request had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the pull request will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@cevich @abitrolly are you still working on this PR?
@vrothberg I think neither of us has an idea how to fix it?  Maybe we should disable snap packaging?

@cevich the right fix is to grab the binaries and pack them into `snap` instead of rebuilding the project from scratch using `snapcraft`. If you pass and echo these binary URLs in `upload_snap` task, I can see what it takes to pass them to `snapcraft`.

@abitrolly thanks for the hint, I simply have to be cautious about where I spent my time.  Currently my automation-related priorities are keeping me busy for the foreseeable feature.  Though I would like to see this effort move forward (in general), and I'm happy to consult as required.

@lsm5 even if you can't, do you know anyone that can help with this?
Perhaps @abitrolly Would like to take a stab.  Hint Hint.
@cevich `make` build fails. What does it need? https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4135/checks?check_run_id=359532249
@abitrolly sorry I missed your mention above.   TBH I'm not sure at all what that means :confused: but it would be worth rebasing this PR against the latest master to see if it reproduces.

@cevich snap uses `go 1.10.4` - could it be the problem?

https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6604298431758336?command=snapcraft#L583

> snap uses go 1.10.4 - could it be the problem?

Easily...along with a whole host of other build-environment issues :disappointed:   Not sure how much trouble it will be (docs may not be accurate): https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/install.md#building-from-scratch

The setup used for preparing ubuntu VM is in: `./contrib/cirrus/packer/ubuntu_setup.sh`
(minus installing the podman package)  There's also a `Dockerfile` which may be more up to date WRT environment setup.

Could either of those be re-used, maybe even with some modifications to work for both usages?
`gate` linter fails on go code and I don't touch it anymore.
There was a problem introduced last week that affected the gate job for everybody.  A fix was pushed to master, so perhaps you just need to rebase this PR?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
This probably needs a rebase too
Friendly ping.

@cevich in ideal world the list of OS level dependencies should be maintained in one central location, such as https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/dependencies.txt and then reused for
* podman.io
* Makefile
* Dockerfile
* snap
* packer
* ansible and other provisioning tools if any are needed for tests

> in ideal world the list of OS level dependencies should be maintained 

Yep, that's the general idea here, except it isn't as simple as a package list.  We need a script because there are occasionally dependency-related actions we need to take depending on different circumstances in time, distro versions, etc.  For example, we found a major bug in BATS and needed to script a workaround for a specific version, since we couldn't wait for an upstream fix.
I can add a container image for Dockerfile.ubuntu and have it auto-build by `quay.io` if that would help you reference by a FROM.
Is this PR still desired?

I failed to run `snapcraft` on Fedora - https://forum.snapcraft.io/t/fedora-32-snapcraft-fail-multipass-timeout/17783/13 - so no progress from my side. `snap` support there is broken. There is no network for some reason. Neither for `snapcraft`, nor for LXD installed from snaps - containers can not ping google and I've tired fighting problems. I can not even run snapcraft with podman, because of SELinux bug with Python `shutil`.
